title: Shakshuka
sources: North African Maghrebi origin (Tunisia, Algeria, Libya). Pre-1928 cookery books on Maghrebi cooking are thin in public-domain English; sources mostly post-date that boundary.
USDA / NCHFP material on egg cookery temperatures is the closest public-domain food-safety reference.
notes: Eggs poached in a tomato-pepper-onion sauce. North African origin via Israel into the global brunch canon. Single skillet, 25 minutes. Sub-30 recipe. Vegetarian. Goes from cupboard ingredients; sturdy weeknight or weekend brunch.
